notes: Regression prompts for the areas fixed in the 2026-07 review. Run each with the skill loaded and check the expected_output holds.

evals.prompt: Create a Business Rule that updates the parent problem's work notes whenever a related incident is created. Use the Fluent SDK — my project has now.config.json with scope x_acme_ops.
evals.expected_output: A BusinessRule .now.ts definition (with $id) whose script body uses GlideRecord/current correctly (before/after timing chosen appropriately). Must NOT refuse GlideRecord in the script body, and must NOT define metadata via GlideRecord.

evals.prompt: now-sdk install failed with 'Could not determine app installation status'. Should I just run it again with --reinstall?
evals.expected_output: Warns that --reinstall is destructive (removes instance metadata not in the local package), requires running transform first and confirming nothing instance-only is lost before using it.

evals.prompt: Write a background script that counts all active P1/P2 incidents per assignment group and logs the totals.
evals.expected_output: Uses GlideAggregate with COUNT and groupBy (not getRowCount in a loop); only real API methods; gs.info logging.

evals.prompt: We're modeling the app 'Atlas CRM' in the CMDB: it has PROD and UAT, runs on two Windows servers with a SQL Server database. Which classes do the records go on?
evals.expected_output: BA on cmdb_ci_business_app; one Application Service per environment on the class the instance standardized on (cmdb_ci_service_auto OOB manual / cmdb_ci_service_discovered where that's the org standard or Service Mapping owns it) — never one catch-all service; servers on cmdb_ci_win_server; DB on cmdb_ci_db_mssql_instance; service-to-CI via svc_ci_assoc. Must not present cmdb_ci_service_auto as 'auto-discovered by Service Mapping'.

evals.prompt: My after-insert business rule sets current.u_processed = true but the field never saves. Why?
evals.expected_output: Explains after rules run post-save so changes to current are lost; fix is a before rule (no update()) — not adding current.update() (recursion). May mention async + separate GlideRecord for other records.

evals.prompt: Is our HR portal a Business Application or just a dependency? It's SaaS, has its own owner in HR, its own budget line, and shows up in demand planning every quarter.
evals.expected_output: Runs the classification decision tree, lands on Business Application (SaaS variant: model minimally — entry point, SSO, integrations, explicit vendor-managed/no internal infra), Decision/Why/Risk format with Next Steps.
